task: I’m getting my car ready before the next cold snap, and I want to make sure I don’t miss anything important. Can you help me put together a winter prep checklist I can work through before I head out?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a practical winter prep checklist you can run through before you leave.

## Winter prep checklist

### 1) Check the car
- [ ] **Tires:** tread is good, pressure is correct for cold temps
- [ ] **Battery:** no slow cranking, no corrosion, battery is not weak
- [ ] **Fluids:**  
  - [ ] engine oil is suitable for cold weather  
  - [ ] coolant/antifreeze level is good  
  - [ ] **washer fluid is winter-rated / freeze-resistant**
- [ ] **Wipers:** replace if streaking or cracking
- [ ] **Lights:** headlights, brake lights, turn signals all work
- [ ] **Brakes:** no pulling, grinding, or warning lights
- [ ] **Heat/defrost:** cabin heat and front/rear defrosters work
- [ ] **Fuel:** keep the tank at least **half full**

### 2) Clear the car completely
- [ ] **Roof snow and ice removed**
- [ ] hood, windows, mirrors, lights cleared
- [ ] wheel wells and tailpipe checked for packed snow
- [ ] windshield fully scraped before driving

### 3) Pack the car
Keep these in the vehicle:
- [ ] ice scraper + snow brush
- [ ] small shovel
- [ ] jumper cables or jump pack
- [ ] flashlight + extra batteries
- [ ] blanket or warm layer
- [ ] gloves, hat, and boots
- [ ] traction aid: sand, cat litter, or traction mats
- [ ] phone charger / power bank
- [ ] basic first-aid kit
- [ ] water and a few snacks
- [ ] warning triangle / reflective vest
- [ ] tow strap if you know how to use it safely

### 4) Before you pull out
- [ ] check the forecast and road conditions
- [ ] allow extra time
- [ ] make sure windows are fully clear
- [ ] set navigation before moving
- [ ] tell someone your route if conditions are rough

### 5) Go / no-go quick filter
**Delay or skip the trip if:**
- visibility is poor
- roads are untreated and slick
- freezing rain / ice is active
- your battery is weak or the car is not starting cleanly
- you can’t fully clear the car
- the route includes bridges/overpasses with icing and conditions are worsening

If you want, I can turn this into a **short yes/no checklist** you can keep in your phone notes or glove box.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. It’s solid and not fluffy.

I’d use it, but I’d tighten two things for real-world use:
- add **tire pressure check when cold**, not just “pressure is correct”
- add **keep washer fluid topped off before the temperature drops**, because people always forget that until it’s slush

The no-go filter is good. That’s the part most people ignore and then act surprised when they end up in a ditch.

If you want, I can turn it into a **one-page pre-drive checklist** with only the stuff that actually matters.
